title: caltha arctica subsp. sibirica
intro: Caltha arctica subsp. sibirica, also known as the Arctic marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to its high content of alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including the reduction of pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Caltha arctica subsp. sibirica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
